description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical wooden utility pole with a single horizontal crossarm mounted near the top. The crossarm supports four insulators, evenly spaced along its length, which are typically used to hold electrical wires. The pole is dark brown, showing a weathered wood texture, and the insulators appear as small, dark, rounded elements on top of the crossarm. There are no visible transformers, streetlights, or identification tags attached to the pole. The structure is robust and designed to be fixed in the ground, serving as a support for overhead power or communication lines. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and function in an urban or roadside environment.
description: A tall, weathered wooden utility pole with a single horizontal crossarm and four insulators, designed to support overhead wires.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the pole, the horizontal crossarm, and all four insulators clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cylindrical shape of the pole and the thin profile of the crossarm, with insulators barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the crossarm and insulators from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the pole's cylindrical form and the crossarm's thin edge.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole.
